contracts.title: bootstrap source of truth is locked
compiler entrypoints are stable
compiler output is deterministic for identical inputs
stage0, stage1 and stage2 bootstrap chain is gated
self-hosting reproducibility is gated
same inputs produce the same build hash
generated compiler files are declared and checked
compiler topology has no orphan module surface
legacy compiler import paths are linted
vitte/vittee/vittec path typos are blocked
CLI diagnostic options are documented and tested
lexer token contract has tests and a gate
lexer participates in fuzz and recovery smoke tests
each declared lexer token has a fixture or token contract proof
lexer errors are cataloged diagnostics
lexer covers ASCII, Unicode, CRLF, LF and tabs
lexer long-line inputs are covered
lexer empty-file behavior is covered
lexer token order is stable
lexer recovers after invalid input without aborting the diagnostic stream
lexer spans preserve byte offsets and visual columns
lexer invalid inputs do not panic
lexer diagnostics have JSON and text snapshots
parser grammar and recovery are gated
parser precedence has property coverage
each grammar rule is backed by grammar fixtures
parser recovery paths have fixtures
parser-produced AST nodes are tested
parser root diagnostics are ordered before cascades
parser diagnostic order is stable
parser has AST snapshot coverage through frontend AST tests
parser diagnostics have text and JSON snapshots
parser suggestions participate in verified counterfactual contracts
parser stray tokens are surfaced by recovery contracts
parser diagnostics cannot fall back to vague catch-all wording
AST surface has tests
AST nodes carry real source spans
AST lowering does not replace real spans with synthetic spans
AST child order is stable
AST attributes valid and invalid placements are covered
AST imports and modules are covered
AST expressions are covered
AST statements are covered
AST patterns are covered
AST types are covered
AST JSON-style snapshot coverage is gated
HIR supported surface is manifest gated
Sema supported surface is manifest gated
Type checker supported surface is manifest gated
Typeck coercions are covered by manifest and production tests
Typeck casts include invalid cast diagnostics and fixtures
Typeck generic inference and substitution are covered
Typeck trait bounds and impl method typing are covered
Typeck diagnostic cause chains are snapshotted and stable
Borrow checker supported surface is manifest gated
Borrowck supported surface is declared in the coverage manifest
Borrowck moves, loans and lifetimes are covered together
Borrowck use-after-move diagnostics are covered by fixture and snapshots
Borrowck shared and mutable borrow conflicts are covered
Borrowck partial move behavior is covered
Borrowck reborrow and alias-path contracts are tracked
Borrowck scope end releases borrow windows
Borrowck closure, async, defer and loop contracts are tracked when supported
Borrowck cause chains describe the chronological move, loan and use sequence
Borrowck diagnostics have JSON and text snapshots
MIR surface, snapshots and validation are gated
MIR supported surface is manifest gated
MIR CFG validity is tested
MIR blocks require terminators
MIR target blocks must exist
MIR predecessor relationships are coherent
MIR function, block, statement and temporary ids are stable
MIR temporary and local counts are tracked
MIR validation runs after pass boundaries
MIR pass runner stops when invalid MIR is produced
MIR has JSON and text snapshots
backend IR is canonical and versioned
IR dialect is explicitly versioned
IR schema_version is part of the public contract
MIR to IR lowering is deterministic
Retired shadow IR modules are forbidden
IR validation rejects invalid units
IR coverage and fixtures have snapshots
Backends consume one canonical IR contract
Compiler stages do not import legacy IR paths directly
IR carries ABI-ready target and linkage metadata
IR golden fixtures are checked
backend contracts and architecture matrix are audited
Backend targets are declared in the architecture matrix
each backend target has a feature matrix entry
x86_64 backend target is declared and audited
AArch64 backend target is declared and audited
RISC-V64 backend target is declared and audited
i386 target configuration is declared and covered
native object generation is audited
backend linking is represented and diagnosed
host toolchain failures produce audited diagnostics
backend command surfaces have snapshots or audit reports
diagnostic catalog is complete and migration-free
diagnostics use one central catalog
diagnostic codes are unique
dynamic diagnostic code construction is forbidden
free-form diagnostics outside ICE are blocked
vague diagnostic terms require a precise cause
each real diagnostic code has a test
each real diagnostic code has a snapshot
each diagnostic carries a source span
JSON, LSP and terminal renderers share the same canonical diagnostic
driver summary reports counts, stopped phase and files without replacing details
suggestions are classified, scored, proven and non-vague
suggestions cannot use vague wording
each suggestion declares a correction strategy
each actionable suggestion carries proof metadata
each suggestion exposes residual risk
each counterfactual suggestion has a public score
automatic suggestions require exact replacements unless manual
automatic fix-its are applied in a copy and recompiled
verified suggestions are rejected when they create a new error
fix application is non-mutating unless the recompiled plan succeeds
fix plans are snapshotted in text and JSON-oriented tests
counterfactual fixes are virtually recompiled and ranked
counterfactual diagnostics generate multiple hypotheses
counterfactual validation applies changes in a virtual program copy
counterfactual validation records lexer, parser, resolver, typeck and borrowck checks
counterfactual fixes are ranked deterministically
counterfactual results count removed errors
counterfactual results count newly created errors
counterfactual outcomes identify moved errors
counterfactual outcomes flag potential behavior changes
counterfactual recompilation cache keys are part of the contract
counterfactual fixes expose a virtual diff
LSP diagnostics and code actions preserve diagnostic proof
LSP diagnostics preserve the same canonical diagnostic as CLI
LSP CodeAction entries carry diagnostic proof metadata
LSP rejected CodeActions include a disabled reason
LSP hover exposes diagnostic explanation context
LSP goto declaration is covered by the protocol gate
LSP rename capability is declared and snapshotted
LSP completion is covered by gate and artifacts
LSP semantic tokens are covered by gate and artifacts
LSP formatting capability is declared and snapshotted
LSP protocol snapshots are generated for core surfaces
CLI diagnostics use the canonical renderer and snapshots
vitte check is the reference diagnostic command
vitte build uses the same diagnostic path before final generation
vitte check and vitte build keep diagnostic parity
--error-format is parsed and tested
--diagnostic-width is parsed and tested
--color auto, always and never are parsed and tested
--max-errors limits diagnostics after detailed output
--deny-warnings is parsed and policy-tested
--allow, --warn and --deny CODE are parsed without disabling structural errors
vitte explain CODE renders full diagnostic explanations
--fix is a parsed fix-plan mode
--fix-safe restricts application to safe verified fixes
--fix-suggested selects verified suggested fixes
--fix-preview prints a virtual diff without mutating files
--fix-why explains fix ranking and residual risk
--fix-plan=json exposes machine-readable fix plans
--max-fix-hypotheses controls counterfactual search breadth
--counterfactual-level selects off, basic, full or exhaustive verification
fix application refuses behavior-changing corrections
fix-it gates recompile after applying automatic replacements
compiler test surfaces are manifested
each supported compiler surface is covered by its gate
valid, invalid and multifile fixtures exist across compiler phases
text, JSON and LSP snapshots are covered
Unicode, CRLF, tabs and long-line diagnostics are covered
compiler fuzz smoke tests are gated
compiler property tests are gated
compiler determinism is smoke-tested
diagnostic and compiler ordering is stable
mutation-style fix application is represented by non-mutating recompile tests
compiler max gate composes core compiler contracts
compiler-max-gate is the top-level compiler contract gate
core-language-gate composes frontend and middle compiler gates
diagnostic-quality is mandatory in compiler CI
contract reports are emitted as machine-readable JSON
